heading: Public access to papers
label: 107 Public access to papers
notes: senate report no. 95–989Subsection (a) of this section makes all papers filed in a bankruptcy case and the dockets of the bankruptcy court public and open to examination at reasonable times without charge. “Docket” includes the claims docket, the proceedings docket, and all papers filed in a case.Subsection (b) permits the court, on its own motion, and requires the court, on the request of a party in interest, to protect trade secrets, confidential research, development, or commercial information, and to protect persons against scandalous or defamatory matter.

children.label: a
children.text: Except as provided in subsections (b) and (c) and subject to section 112, a paper filed in a case under this title and the dockets of a bankruptcy court are public records and open to examination by an entity at reasonable times without charge.
protect an entity with respect to a trade secret or confidential research, development, or commercial information; or
protect a person with respect to scandalous or defamatory matter contained in a paper filed in a case under this title.

children.label: A
children.text: Any means of identification (as defined in ) contained in a paper filed, or to be filed, in a case under this title.section 1028(d) of title 18

children.label: B
children.text: Other information contained in a paper described in subparagraph (A).

Upon ex parte application demonstrating cause, the court shall provide access to information protected pursuant to paragraph (1) to an entity acting pursuant to the police or regulatory power of a domestic governmental unit.

children.label: A
children.text: shall have full access to all information contained in any paper filed or submitted in a case under this title; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall not disclose information specifically protected by the court under this title.
